vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data is severely compromised by extreme underexposure (near-total darkness). Only the yellow pot (P1) and the black pot containing the Pothos/Rabbit anchor (P3) are partially discernible. P2 and P4 are effectively invisible.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot appears empty or contains only soil; no foliage visible.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen is present; the white rabbit anchor is visible as a high-contrast object in the center of the pot.
plant_audit.P2_P4: No biological material detected due to lighting failure.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Indeterminate due to low light; appears dark and potentially moist.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: The primary anomaly is the 'Blackout State' of the environment, rendering standard botanical assessment impossible.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a consistent lack of light. No growth or decay can be tracked as the specimens remain obscured in shadow across all frames.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The health status is 'Indeterminate'. The lack of visible chlorophyll-bearing tissue in P1 and the inability to see P2/P4 prevents a clinical diagnosis. P3 appears structurally intact but lacks visible leaf detail.
anomalies: Total loss of visibility for P2 and P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit was performed by first cataloging the visible pots against the registry, then attempting to track changes across the temporal stack. Validation confirms that the lighting conditions are insufficient for a professional botanical assessment. The Pothos (P3) remains the only identifiable specimen, while the other registry items are effectively lost to the darkness of the imaging environment.
